---
title: "R Learning Path"
format: html
---

Return to R by learning the work you need to do, not every corner of the language.

```{=html}
<style>
  *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box; }

  :root {
    --bg: #f6f4ee;
    --panel: rgba(255,255,255,0.86);
    --panel-strong: #fffdfa;
    --text: #1d1f1a;
    --muted: #65685f;
    --subtle: #8c8f85;
    --line: rgba(24, 32, 20, 0.12);
    --line-strong: rgba(24, 32, 20, 0.22);
    --accent: #155c48;
    --accent-soft: #dbeee7;
    --accent-2: #a54426;
    --accent-2-soft: #f8e9e2;
    --accent-3: #1e5f92;
    --accent-3-soft: #e3eef8;
    --accent-4: #8b6311;
    --accent-4-soft: #f8ecd3;
    --shadow: 0 18px 50px rgba(42, 49, 36, 0.08);
    --radius: 22px;
    --font: "Segoe UI", "Aptos", system-ui, sans-serif;
  }

  @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark) {
    :root {
      --bg: #1b1d19;
      --panel: rgba(34, 37, 32, 0.92);
      --panel-strong: #242822;
      --text: #eceadf;
      --muted: #bcc0b4;
      --subtle: #8f9388;
      --line: rgba(255,255,255,0.10);
      --line-strong: rgba(255,255,255,0.20);
      --accent: #8ed1b8;
      --accent-soft: #113e31;
      --accent-2: #f1a183;
      --accent-2-soft: #4d1f10;
      --accent-3: #9bc6ec;
      --accent-3-soft: #123652;
      --accent-4: #f0c66a;
      --accent-4-soft: #493104;
      --shadow: none;
    }
  }

  body {
    margin: 0;
    font-family: var(--font);
    color: var(--text);
    background:
      radial-gradient(circle at top left, rgba(21, 92, 72, 0.10), transparent 28%),
      radial-gradient(circle at top right, rgba(165, 68, 38, 0.08), transparent 24%),
      linear-gradient(180deg, #fbfaf6 0%, var(--bg) 32%, var(--bg) 100%);
    min-height: 100vh;
    padding: 2rem 1rem 3rem;
  }

  .page-shell {
    max-width: 1180px;
    margin: 0 auto;
  }

  .hero {
    background: var(--panel);
    border: 1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius: 28px;
    box-shadow: var(--shadow);
    padding: 2rem;
    margin-bottom: 1.4rem;
    backdrop-filter: blur(10px);
  }

  .eyebrow {
    display: inline-flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 0.45rem;
    padding: 0.35rem 0.7rem;
    border-radius: 999px;
    background: var(--accent-soft);
    color: var(--accent);
    font-size: 0.76rem;
    font-weight: 700;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 0.08em;
  }

  .hero h1 {
    font-size: clamp(2rem, 4vw, 3.35rem);
    line-height: 1.02;
    margin: 0.95rem 0 0.8rem;
    max-width: 14ch;
  }

  .hero p.lead {
    font-size: 1.05rem;
    line-height: 1.7;
    color: var(--muted);
    max-width: 74ch;
    margin: 0 0 1.2rem;
  }

  .hero-grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: 2.2fr 1fr;
    gap: 1rem;
    align-items: start;
  }

  .hero-note {
    background: var(--panel-strong);
    border: 1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius: 18px;
    padding: 1rem 1.05rem;
  }

  .hero-note h2 {
    margin: 0 0 0.5rem;
    font-size: 0.92rem;
  }

  .hero-note p {
    margin: 0;
    font-size: 0.9rem;
    line-height: 1.6;
    color: var(--muted);
  }

  .summary-strip {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(3, minmax(0, 1fr));
    gap: 0.9rem;
    margin-top: 1rem;
  }

  .summary-card {
    background: var(--panel-strong);
    border: 1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius: 18px;
    padding: 1rem;
  }

  .summary-card .kicker {
    display: block;
    font-size: 0.74rem;
    letter-spacing: 0.08em;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    color: var(--subtle);
    margin-bottom: 0.45rem;
    font-weight: 700;
  }

  .summary-card p {
    margin: 0;
    color: var(--muted);
    font-size: 0.92rem;
    line-height: 1.55;
  }

  .journey {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: 340px minmax(0, 1fr);
    gap: 1rem;
    align-items: start;
  }

  .journey-nav,
  .journey-panel-wrap {
    background: var(--panel);
    border: 1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius: 28px;
    box-shadow: var(--shadow);
    backdrop-filter: blur(10px);
  }

  .journey-nav {
    padding: 1.15rem;
    position: sticky;
    top: 1rem;
  }

  .section-title {
    margin: 0 0 0.35rem;
    font-size: 0.78rem;
    font-weight: 700;
    letter-spacing: 0.09em;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    color: var(--subtle);
  }

  .section-copy {
    margin: 0 0 1rem;
    font-size: 0.92rem;
    color: var(--muted);
    line-height: 1.55;
  }

  .stage-list {
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 0.8rem;
  }

  .stage-btn {
    width: 100%;
    border: 1px solid var(--line);
    background: var(--panel-strong);
    border-radius: 20px;
    text-align: left;
    padding: 0.95rem;
    cursor: pointer;
    transition: transform 0.15s ease, border-color 0.15s ease, background 0.15s ease;
  }

  .stage-btn:hover {
    transform: translateY(-1px);
    border-color: var(--line-strong);
  }

  .stage-btn.active {
    background: linear-gradient(135deg, var(--accent-soft), var(--panel-strong));
    border-color: rgba(21, 92, 72, 0.35);
  }

  .stage-index {
    display: inline-flex;
    width: 1.8rem;
    height: 1.8rem;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    border-radius: 50%;
    background: var(--accent);
    color: #fff;
    font-size: 0.82rem;
    font-weight: 700;
    margin-bottom: 0.7rem;
  }

  .stage-btn.active .stage-index {
    background: var(--accent-2);
  }

  .stage-title {
    display: block;
    font-size: 1rem;
    font-weight: 700;
    color: var(--text);
    margin-bottom: 0.25rem;
  }

  .stage-sub {
    display: block;
    font-size: 0.86rem;
    color: var(--muted);
    line-height: 1.45;
  }

  .journey-panel-wrap {
    padding: 1.35rem;
  }

  .panel {
    display: none;
  }

  .panel.active {
    display: block;
  }

  .panel-head {
    display: flex;
    align-items: flex-start;
    justify-content: space-between;
    gap: 1rem;
    margin-bottom: 1rem;
  }

  .pill {
    display: inline-block;
    padding: 0.34rem 0.68rem;
    border-radius: 999px;
    font-size: 0.72rem;
    letter-spacing: 0.08em;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    font-weight: 700;
  }

  .pill-green { background: var(--accent-soft); color: var(--accent); }
  .pill-coral { background: var(--accent-2-soft); color: var(--accent-2); }
  .pill-blue  { background: var(--accent-3-soft); color: var(--accent-3); }
  .pill-amber { background: var(--accent-4-soft); color: var(--accent-4); }

  .panel h2 {
    margin: 0.6rem 0 0;
    font-size: clamp(1.55rem, 3vw, 2.2rem);
    line-height: 1.1;
  }

  .panel-intro {
    margin: 0 0 1rem;
    font-size: 1rem;
    line-height: 1.7;
    color: var(--muted);
    max-width: 68ch;
  }

  .close-btn {
    border: 1px solid var(--line);
    background: var(--panel-strong);
    color: var(--muted);
    border-radius: 12px;
    cursor: pointer;
    padding: 0.5rem 0.7rem;
    font-size: 0.84rem;
  }

  .panel-grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr));
    gap: 0.95rem;
  }

  .content-card {
    background: var(--panel-strong);
    border: 1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius: 20px;
    padding: 1rem;
    min-height: 100%;
  }

  .content-card h3 {
    margin: 0 0 0.55rem;
    font-size: 0.92rem;
  }

  .content-card p {
    margin: 0;
    color: var(--muted);
    font-size: 0.92rem;
    line-height: 1.65;
  }

  .content-card ul {
    margin: 0;
    padding-left: 1.1rem;
    color: var(--muted);
  }

  .content-card li {
    margin: 0 0 0.5rem;
    line-height: 1.55;
  }

  .prompt-list {
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 0.65rem;
  }

  .prompt {
    border-left: 4px solid var(--accent);
    background: rgba(21, 92, 72, 0.06);
    padding: 0.85rem 0.9rem;
    border-radius: 0 14px 14px 0;
    font-size: 0.91rem;
    color: var(--text);
    line-height: 1.55;
  }

  .resource-list {
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 0.7rem;
  }

  .resource-card {
    display: block;
    text-decoration: none;
    color: inherit;
    border: 1px solid var(--line);
    background: var(--panel-strong);
    border-radius: 16px;
    padding: 0.9rem;
    transition: border-color 0.15s ease, transform 0.15s ease;
  }

  .resource-card:hover {
    border-color: var(--line-strong);
    transform: translateY(-1px);
  }

  .resource-card strong {
    display: block;
    margin-bottom: 0.18rem;
    font-size: 0.94rem;
  }

  .resource-card span {
    display: block;
    color: var(--muted);
    font-size: 0.87rem;
    line-height: 1.5;
  }

  .resource-card em {
    display: block;
    margin-top: 0.4rem;
    color: var(--subtle);
    font-size: 0.75rem;
    font-style: normal;
  }

  .panel-foot {
    margin-top: 1rem;
    padding: 1rem 1.05rem;
    border-radius: 20px;
    background: linear-gradient(135deg, rgba(30, 95, 146, 0.08), rgba(21, 92, 72, 0.08));
    border: 1px solid var(--line);
    color: var(--muted);
    font-size: 0.93rem;
    line-height: 1.65;
  }

  @media (max-width: 980px) {
    .hero-grid,
    .journey,
    .panel-grid,
    .summary-strip {
      grid-template-columns: 1fr;
    }

    .journey-nav {
      position: static;
    }
  }
</style>

<div class="page-shell">
  <section class="hero">
    <span class="eyebrow">Clinical-trial analysts returning to R</span>
    <div class="hero-grid">
      <div>
        <h1>Use AI to get back into R faster.</h1>
        <p class="lead">
          If you last touched R years ago at university, the fastest route back is not a long course taken in order.
          It is a guided, applied path: learn enough syntax to recognise what R is doing, use AI to translate what you
          already know from SAS, SPSS, Excel, or other analysis workflows, then learn from real scripts and real tasks.
        </p>
      </div>
      <aside class="hero-note">
        <h2>What this page is trying to do</h2>
        <p>
          Build confidence first, then capability. You do not need to master R before being useful in phase 1 to phase 4
          clinical-trial work. You need a workflow that helps you start, understand, debug, and improve.
        </p>
      </aside>
    </div>

    <div class="summary-strip">
      <div class="summary-card">
        <span class="kicker">Mindset</span>
        <p>Do not begin with "learn all of R." Begin with the analysis work you actually need to do.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="summary-card">
        <span class="kicker">AI Role</span>
        <p>Use AI as a translator, explainer, code reviewer, and debugging partner while you learn in context.</p>
      </div>
      <div class="summary-card">
        <span class="kicker">Audience</span>
        <p>Designed for people returning to R and working in clinical development, not only health economics.</p>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <section class="journey">
    <aside class="journey-nav">
      <p class="section-title">Recommended path</p>
      <p class="section-copy">Start at the top and move stage by stage. You can revisit any stage whenever a real project exposes a gap.</p>

      <div class="stage-list">
        <button class="stage-btn active" id="btn-orientation" onclick="showStage('orientation')">
          <span class="stage-index">1</span>
          <span class="stage-title">Orientation</span>
          <span class="stage-sub">You are translating existing analytical thinking, not starting from zero.</span>
        </button>

        <button class="stage-btn" id="btn-syntax" onclick="showStage('syntax')">
          <span class="stage-index">2</span>
          <span class="stage-title">Learn Enough Syntax</span>
          <span class="stage-sub">Recognise data frames, dplyr, ggplot2, pipes, and packages without aiming for mastery yet.</span>
        </button>

        <button class="stage-btn" id="btn-ai" onclick="showStage('ai')">
          <span class="stage-index">3</span>
          <span class="stage-title">Use AI as a Translator</span>
          <span class="stage-sub">Turn familiar SAS, SPSS, Excel, or analysis ideas into R code and explanations.</span>
        </button>

        <button class="stage-btn" id="btn-code" onclick="showStage('code')">
          <span class="stage-index">4</span>
          <span class="stage-title">Learn From Real Code</span>
          <span class="stage-sub">Study the scripts and package patterns your team actually uses.</span>
        </button>

        <button class="stage-btn" id="btn-workflow" onclick="showStage('workflow')">
          <span class="stage-index">5</span>
          <span class="stage-title">Build an AI Workflow</span>
          <span class="stage-sub">Plan in English, generate code, run it, debug it, and ask AI to explain the final version.</span>
        </button>

        <button class="stage-btn" id="btn-trials" onclick="showStage('trials')">
          <span class="stage-index">6</span>
          <span class="stage-title">Clinical-Trial Next Steps</span>
          <span class="stage-sub">Grow into reproducible analysis, reporting, and trial-focused package choices.</span>
        </button>
      </div>
    </aside>

    <div class="journey-panel-wrap">
      <div class="panel active" id="panel-orientation">
        <div class="panel-head">
          <div>
            <span class="pill pill-green">Stage 1</span>
            <h2>Orientation: start from what you already know</h2>
          </div>
          <button class="close-btn" onclick="showStage('orientation')">Reset stage</button>
        </div>
        <p class="panel-intro">
          The goal is not to become an R expert before you begin. The goal is to make your existing analysis experience useful in a new language and workflow.
        </p>
        <div class="panel-grid">
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Goal</h3>
            <p>Replace the feeling of "I have to relearn everything" with "I already know the analysis, I just need to learn how R expresses it."</p>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>What to do</h3>
            <ul>
              <li>Accept that returning to R is mostly a translation problem, not a statistics problem.</li>
              <li>Pick one real task you care about, such as summarising data, creating a plot, or preparing an output table.</li>
              <li>Use this page as a roadmap, not a syllabus you must finish perfectly.</li>
            </ul>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Ask AI this</h3>
            <div class="prompt-list">
              <div class="prompt">"I last used R years ago at university. I already know statistics and clinical-trial analysis. What are the minimum R ideas I need to get productive again?"</div>
              <div class="prompt">"Help me build a simple learning plan for returning to R when I already understand analysis concepts but not modern R workflows."</div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Why it matters in clinical trials</h3>
            <p>In phase 1 to phase 4 work, the challenge is rarely the analysis logic alone. The challenge is moving confidently between datasets, summaries, plots, scripts, and reproducible workflows.</p>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div class="panel-foot">If this stage works, the learner should feel less pressure to "study everything first" and more ready to begin with practical tasks.</div>
      </div>

      <div class="panel" id="panel-syntax">
        <div class="panel-head">
          <div>
            <span class="pill pill-blue">Stage 2</span>
            <h2>Learn enough syntax to recognise what R is doing</h2>
          </div>
          <button class="close-btn" onclick="showStage('orientation')">Back to start</button>
        </div>
        <p class="panel-intro">
          Spend a short burst of time on the core shapes of modern R code. The aim is recognition and confidence, not deep mastery.
        </p>
        <div class="panel-grid">
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Goal</h3>
            <p>Become comfortable seeing data frames, verbs like <code>filter()</code> and <code>mutate()</code>, pipes, and basic <code>ggplot2</code> code.</p>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>What to do</h3>
            <ul>
              <li>Spend one or two days on introductory material only.</li>
              <li>Focus on data frames, <code>dplyr</code>, <code>ggplot2</code>, pipes, and packages.</li>
              <li>Do not worry if you cannot write everything from memory.</li>
            </ul>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Ask AI this</h3>
            <div class="prompt-list">
              <div class="prompt">"Explain data frames, dplyr, ggplot2, and pipes as if I am a clinical-trial analyst returning to R."</div>
              <div class="prompt">"Show me a tiny example that reads a dataset, filters rows, creates a new variable, and makes a simple plot in R."</div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Recommended resources</h3>
            <div class="resource-list">
              <a class="resource-card" href="https://gsk-biostatistics.github.io/intro_to_r_and_the_tidyverse_training/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
                <strong>GSK: Intro to R and the tidyverse</strong>
                <span>Practical, structured material for getting comfortable with modern R syntax.</span>
                <em>gsk-biostatistics.github.io</em>
              </a>
              <a class="resource-card" href="https://r4ds.hadley.nz/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
                <strong>R for Data Science</strong>
                <span>Use selected sections, not necessarily front-to-back, to recognise common tidyverse patterns.</span>
                <em>r4ds.hadley.nz</em>
              </a>
              <a class="resource-card" href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yZ0bV2Afkjc" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
                <strong>Introductory video walkthrough</strong>
                <span>A lightweight way to re-familiarise yourself with what R code looks like in practice.</span>
                <em>youtube.com</em>
              </a>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div class="panel-foot">If someone can look at a short tidyverse script and roughly follow its shape, this stage has done its job.</div>
      </div>

      <div class="panel" id="panel-ai">
        <div class="panel-head">
          <div>
            <span class="pill pill-coral">Stage 3</span>
            <h2>Use AI as a translator, not just a search engine</h2>
          </div>
          <button class="close-btn" onclick="showStage('orientation')">Back to start</button>
        </div>
        <p class="panel-intro">
          AI is most useful when you ask it to bridge from what you already know into R code, explanation, and debugging help.
        </p>
        <div class="panel-grid">
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Goal</h3>
            <p>Learn in context by translating familiar analytical tasks into R instead of trying to memorise syntax in isolation.</p>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>What to do</h3>
            <ul>
              <li>Describe the task the way you would describe it in SAS, SPSS, Excel, or plain English.</li>
              <li>Ask AI to explain code line by line, not only to generate code.</li>
              <li>When you hit an error, paste the code and error together and ask for diagnosis.</li>
            </ul>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Ask AI this</h3>
            <div class="prompt-list">
              <div class="prompt">"I would do this in SAS. How do I do it in R using tidyverse code?"</div>
              <div class="prompt">"Explain this R code line by line as if I am an analyst new to R."</div>
              <div class="prompt">"I ran this code and got this error. Tell me what the error means, what probably caused it, and how to fix it."</div>
              <div class="prompt">"Rewrite this R code in a more beginner-friendly way and explain the packages being used."</div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Recommended resources</h3>
            <div class="resource-list">
              <a class="resource-card" href="https://posit.cloud/learn/recipes" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
                <strong>Posit Recipes</strong>
                <span>Bite-sized examples that pair well with AI because each task is small and concrete.</span>
                <em>posit.cloud/learn/recipes</em>
              </a>
              <a class="resource-card" href="https://posit.co/resources/cheatsheets/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
                <strong>Posit cheat sheets</strong>
                <span>Useful as a quick reference while AI helps interpret and apply the patterns.</span>
                <em>posit.co/resources/cheatsheets</em>
              </a>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div class="panel-foot">This stage is where learning speed usually jumps. AI lets people learn from real work instead of waiting until they feel fully trained.</div>
      </div>

      <div class="panel" id="panel-code">
        <div class="panel-head">
          <div>
            <span class="pill pill-green">Stage 4</span>
            <h2>Learn from the real code your team already uses</h2>
          </div>
          <button class="close-btn" onclick="showStage('orientation')">Back to start</button>
        </div>
        <p class="panel-intro">
          The most relevant R education often comes from the scripts, packages, and conventions already present in your team.
        </p>
        <div class="panel-grid">
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Goal</h3>
            <p>Understand the actual style of R code used around you, not just generic examples from tutorials.</p>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>What to do</h3>
            <ul>
              <li>Take short sections of shared scripts and study them with AI.</li>
              <li>Notice the packages, naming patterns, and data-flow style your team prefers.</li>
              <li>Ask for beginner-friendly rewrites when a script feels too dense.</li>
            </ul>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Ask AI this</h3>
            <div class="prompt-list">
              <div class="prompt">"Explain what this code is doing and tell me which R concepts I need to understand first."</div>
              <div class="prompt">"What packages are being used here and why would a clinical-trial team choose them?"</div>
              <div class="prompt">"Rewrite this in a more beginner-friendly way but keep the same analytical result."</div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Recommended resources</h3>
            <div class="resource-list">
              <a class="resource-card" href="https://pharmaverse.github.io/intro-to-r-for-sas-programmers-workshop/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
                <strong>pharmaverse: Intro to R for SAS programmers</strong>
                <span>Especially useful when the learner already thinks in SAS-style data and reporting tasks.</span>
                <em>pharmaverse.github.io</em>
              </a>
              <a class="resource-card" href="https://gsk-biostatistics.github.io/intro_to_r_and_the_tidyverse_training/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
                <strong>GSK training materials</strong>
                <span>Helpful as a bridge between structured learning and the code patterns seen in practice.</span>
                <em>gsk-biostatistics.github.io</em>
              </a>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div class="panel-foot">This is often where R starts to feel real: the learner can connect tutorial ideas to the scripts and outputs their team actually cares about.</div>
      </div>

      <div class="panel" id="panel-workflow">
        <div class="panel-head">
          <div>
            <span class="pill pill-amber">Stage 5</span>
            <h2>Build a repeatable AI-assisted coding workflow</h2>
          </div>
          <button class="close-btn" onclick="showStage('orientation')">Back to start</button>
        </div>
        <p class="panel-intro">
          Every analysis task can become a learning loop if you use AI consistently at the right points.
        </p>
        <div class="panel-grid">
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Goal</h3>
            <p>Create a workflow that turns planning, coding, debugging, and explanation into one connected learning cycle.</p>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>What to do</h3>
            <ul>
              <li>Write the analysis plan in plain English first.</li>
              <li>Ask AI to generate a first draft of the R code.</li>
              <li>Run it, inspect the output, then debug with AI when needed.</li>
              <li>Ask AI to explain the final script after it works.</li>
            </ul>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Ask AI this</h3>
            <div class="prompt-list">
              <div class="prompt">"Here is the analysis I need to do. Turn this English plan into tidyverse R code."</div>
              <div class="prompt">"Here is my current script and output. What should I check next?"</div>
              <div class="prompt">"Now that this works, explain the final code and point out the R ideas I should remember."</div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Clinical-trial relevance</h3>
            <p>This mirrors real analytical work: define the dataset question, generate code, check summaries and plots, refine, and keep the final script understandable enough to revisit later.</p>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div class="panel-foot">The long-term aim is not dependence on AI. It is faster understanding, cleaner iteration, and better transfer from one task to the next.</div>
      </div>

      <div class="panel" id="panel-trials">
        <div class="panel-head">
          <div>
            <span class="pill pill-blue">Stage 6</span>
            <h2>Grow into broader clinical-trial R work</h2>
          </div>
          <button class="close-btn" onclick="showStage('orientation')">Back to start</button>
        </div>
        <p class="panel-intro">
          Once the learner can read and adapt code, the next step is to deepen the parts of R that matter most for their role in phase 1 to phase 4 studies.
        </p>
        <div class="panel-grid">
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Goal</h3>
            <p>Move from basic comfort with R into reproducible analysis, reporting, visualisation, and trial-relevant packages.</p>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>What to learn next</h3>
            <ul>
              <li><code>tidyverse</code> for data manipulation and readable scripts</li>
              <li><code>ggplot2</code> for clear visualisation</li>
              <li><code>survival</code> and related methods when the analysis calls for time-to-event work</li>
              <li><code>targets</code> or other reproducible workflows if the team values pipeline structure</li>
              <li>Quarto for reports and shareable outputs</li>
            </ul>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Ask AI this</h3>
            <div class="prompt-list">
              <div class="prompt">"Based on my work in clinical trials, which R packages should I learn next and in what order?"</div>
              <div class="prompt">"Show me how this clinical-trial analysis could be made more reproducible in R."</div>
              <div class="prompt">"What should I learn after dplyr and ggplot2 if I want to create reusable reporting workflows?"</div>
            </div>
          </div>
          <div class="content-card">
            <h3>Recommended resources</h3>
            <div class="resource-list">
              <a class="resource-card" href="https://posit.co/resources/cheatsheets/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
                <strong>Posit cheat sheets</strong>
                <span>Keep these close as you move from learning syntax to building repeatable analysis habits.</span>
                <em>posit.co/resources/cheatsheets</em>
              </a>
              <a class="resource-card" href="https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/markdown-basics.html" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
                <strong>Quarto basics</strong>
                <span>Useful when the learner starts turning analysis into reports, notes, and shareable output.</span>
                <em>quarto.org</em>
              </a>
              <a class="resource-card" href="https://www.tidyverse.org/" target="_blank" rel="noopener">
                <strong>Tidyverse</strong>
                <span>The modern package ecosystem most learners will keep returning to in daily work.</span>
                <em>tidyverse.org</em>
              </a>
            </div>
          </div>
        </div>
        <div class="panel-foot">Health economics can still fit here, but this final stage keeps the page broad enough for analysts across the wider clinical-trial space.</div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </section>
</div>

<script>
const stages = ['orientation', 'syntax', 'ai', 'code', 'workflow', 'trials'];

function showStage(id) {
  stages.forEach(stage => {
    document.getElementById('panel-' + stage).classList.remove('active');
    document.getElementById('btn-' + stage).classList.remove('active');
  });

  document.getElementById('panel-' + id).classList.add('active');
  document.getElementById('btn-' + id).classList.add('active');
}
</script>
```
